sutta: the buddha said monks by increasing in in by increasing in 10 growths the aryan disciple grows in the aryan growth takes whole of the essential takes hold of the best for his person what then he grows in landed property in wealth and greenery in child and wife in slaves and folk who work for him in 4-footed beasts he grows in faith and virtue in love generosity and wisdom in these 10 growths the aryan disciple grows in the aryan growth takes hold of the essential takes hold of the best for his person that's the end of the suta
commentary: this suta is more for lay people they people like you the buddha says these 10 things are good for you in the aryan discipline having more landed property that means having more land property second 1 having wealth and greenery not having a lot of money and and property but of course you must know how to use it properly otherwise uh it would not be good then number 3 child and wife having a lot of children and maybe a lot of wives are so like during the buddha's time because at that time they they they were not monogamous like a man could have as many wives as he could support that then number 4 slaves and work work uh workers number 5 is 4 footed beasts like uh animal property like cattle and pigs and all that number 6 uh having faith having more faith number 7 virtue or moral conduct number 8 love love or what they call it loving kindness number 9 generosity or charity number 10 wisdom so these are good qualities to acquire and good things to acquire for lay people now we come to suta number
